competitors.name: Conagra Brands (Birds Eye, Marie Callender's)
competitors.action: Committed to eliminating all petroleum-based synthetic color additives (Red 40, Yellow 5/6, Blue 1/2, Green 3) from entire U.S. frozen product portfolio by end of 2027. No school sales of affected products starting 2026-2027 school year. Status: in progress as of Dec 2025.
competitors.notes: MAHA-driven reformulation. Large incumbents forced to catch up to clean-label standard Real Dough already meets. Levels the ingredient story for mass brands — Real Dough should proactively highlight its current clean-label status before this becomes table stakes.

competitors.name: Amy's Kitchen
competitors.action: 37 products (including frozen pizzas, entrees, burritos) now carry Non-UPF Verified certification — capitalizing on MAHA/anti-ultra-processed-food movement ahead of the 2026-2027 Dietary Guidelines cycle.
competitors.notes: Amy's is owning the 'not ultra-processed' narrative in frozen. Real Dough's sourdough fermentation + clean ingredients qualifies — worth exploring Non-UPF Verified or equivalent certification as a shelf differentiator.

competitors.name: Screamin' Sicilian
competitors.action: BOGO promotion at multiple grocery retailers (WRAL grocery sales listing) for week of March 25-31, 2026. Also ranked #3 in recent frozen pizza taste test (Daily Voice NJ) for Bessie's Revenge cheese.
competitors.notes: BOGO promotions signal push to move volume. Taste test visibility keeps brand top-of-mind. Monitor frequency — heavy discounting could indicate distribution pressure.

trends.topic: MAHA / anti-synthetic-dye regulatory pressure on frozen CPG
trends.magnitude: FDA tracking industry pledges; Conagra, Kellanova, IDFA committing to reformulation by 2027. California AB 2244 creating 'California Certified' non-UPF seal.
trends.context: Federal MAHA initiative + Trump-era FDA pushing phase-out of 6 petroleum-based food dyes by 2027. Frozen pizza incumbents (Conagra, Nestlé) now forced to reformulate. Real Dough's clean-label, fermented-crust positioning is a first-mover advantage — but window to claim it closes as big brands catch up by 2027.
